title: Validate maia-kmod crash fix on nano hardware

questions.prompt: How many reboot cycles to call the fix verified?
questions.description: The primary test: flash patched firmware, reboot N times, check no free_pgtables panic in kernel log.

questions.prompt: What test programs exercise recording + rxbuffer paths?
questions.description: After boot, we need to confirm mmap/cacheinv/close work without crash. What userspace tools do you use (maia-sdr test programs, custom scripts, etc.)?
questions.recommendedAnswer: maia-sdr upstream test programs (test_recording, test_rxbuffer) or minimal open/close + ioctl loop

questions.prompt: Build firmware locally or via CI?
questions.description: The patch needs to be baked into a firmware image. Local build is ~30min cold; CI is hands-off.
questions.recommendedAnswer: CI (gh workflow run main.yml -f boards=nano) — fast, cached, no local build setup needed

options.label: CI — gh workflow run, download artifact

questions.description: Gotchas, prerequisites, test environment setup, etc.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Nano is QSPI-only. Deploy via pluto.frm on MSD volume or dfu-util.
